prompt: I want to design a SaaS product login page — give me 3 style directions to compare
expected: Triggers clarifying questions about design context/brand; produces a design_canvas with 3 variations; avoids AI slop like purple gradients/emoji/Inter; gives concrete reasoning for the differentiation dimensions of each variation
tests: Workflow asks questions + variations logic + anti-AI-slop checklist + design_canvas usage

prompt: Help me build a 10-page product pitch deck about an AI-tool startup project
expected: Starts with deck_stage.js; first verbally vocalizes the design system (color/typography/layout rhythm) and waits for confirmation; alternates between Section divider/content/data/quote layouts; font size ≥24px; 1-indexed labels
tests: Junior Designer reports before doing + deck_stage usage + visual rhythm + scale conventions

prompt: Make a 30-second HTML animation explaining how neural networks work
expected: Uses Stage+Sprite from animations.jsx; writes the timeline before the components; entrance easeOut, exit easeIn; tells a story in phases instead of stacking animations; text dwells ≥3 seconds
tests: Animations workflow + correct easing + rhythm design + duration control

prompt: Build a Habit Tracker App prototype
expected: Asks the user whether they want overview tiling or flow demo (defaults to overview); uses assets/ios_frame.jsx, doesn't hand-code the Dynamic Island; Tracker is a high-density type, each screen has ≥3 information-density elements (habit completion rate, streak days, trend curves, achievement badges, etc., not decorative); at least 5–7 screens side by side (home/new habit/detail/stats/settings)
tests: Overview/flow form routing + ios_frame hard binding + information density typing (high-density) + multi-screen side-by-side

prompt: Build a Reading Notes App prototype
expected: Overview tiling primary; ios_frame.jsx; reading notes lean toward content-display, density requirement isn't as extreme as Tracker, but the notes list page still needs ≥3 layers of information (book, quote, tags, progress); at least 4–6 screens (home bookshelf/note detail/annotation highlights/search/notebook management); typography prefers serif display
tests: Overview default + ios_frame + information hierarchy + content-first visual rhythm

prompt: Build a Running Tracker App prototype
expected: Overview tiling; ios_frame.jsx; running App is a high-density type (map, pace curve, heart rate zones, per-kilometer split data), each screen has ≥3 product-differentiating pieces of information; at least 5 screens (today's overview/live data while running/route map/history/monthly stats); avoid AI slop (no purple gradients, no piles of decorative icons — but data-viz icons are allowed)
tests: Overview + ios_frame + high-density data visualization + map/chart mixed layout + slop boundary conditions
